title: Spelling & Word Study
objective: Students will explore spelling & word study concepts including Spelling Verb Endings, The Prefix un-, Tricky words and more.

steps.content: Welcome to an exciting lesson about Spelling Verb Endings!
Spell words with common suffixes (-s/-es for plurals and third person verbs, -ing, -ed, -er, -est) where no change to the root word is needed
Let me show you something cool. Add -s or -es correctly to make plurals (e.g. 'cats', 'boxes')
When you writes about more than one dog or talks about something that is happening now, do they add the right ending — like "dogs" or "jumping" — without making spelling mistakes?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Spelling Verb Endings. Add -s or -es correctly to make plurals (e.g. 'cats', 'boxes') Spell words with -ing and -ed endings (e.g. 'jumping', 'helped')
Let's discover the amazing world of The Prefix un-!
Spell words using the prefix un- to change meaning; understand that un- creates opposites or reverses actions
Time to try this out: Spell words with un- prefix correctly (e.g. 'unhappy', 'unfair', 'undo')
Does you know that adding "un-" to the front of a word like "happy" or "lock" flips its meaning, and can they spell those words correctly?
Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about The Prefix un-. Spell words with un- prefix correctly (e.g. 'unhappy', 'unfair', 'undo') Explain how un- changes the meaning of a word
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Tricky words!
Spell common exception words from memory that do not follow regular phonic patterns, including the days of the week
Time to try this out: Correctly spell high-frequency irregular words (e.g. 'said', 'the', 'was')
Can you spell tricky everyday words like "said," "were," "come," and the days of the week from memory without needing to sound them out?
Great job! Today we learned about Tricky words. Correctly spell high-frequency irregular words (e.g. 'said', 'the', 'was') Write all seven days of the week correctly
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Spelling from Dictation!
Write simple sentences from memory when dictated by the teacher, applying taught GPCs, spelling rules, and common exception words
Let's practice together! Write a dictated sentence using phonically plausible spellings
If you read a sentence out loud to you and ask them to write it down, can they spell the words correctly and put the letters in the right order?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Spelling from Dictation. Write a dictated sentence using phonically plausible spellings Include correct spelling of taught exception words in dictation
